title: Sale of Immovable Property
description: A sale is a transfer of ownership in exchange for a price paid or promised. For land above Rs.100, a registered written document is mandatory.
advice: Always verify title at the Sub-Registrar office. Ensure encumbrance certificate is obtained for last 30 years. Get sale deed registered mandatorily.

title: Lease of Immovable Property
description: A lease is a transfer of the right to enjoy property for a specific time in return for rent. Leases above 11 months must be registered.
advice: Leases above 11 months must be registered. Document rent receipts always. Ensure termination notice period is clearly defined.

title: Contract of Loan / Borrowing
description: A loan agreement defines the principal amount, interest rate, repayment schedule, and consequences of default between lender and borrower.
advice: Always get loan agreements notarized. Ensure interest rate complies with RBI guidelines. Keep copies of all repayment receipts.

title: General / Special Power of Attorney
description: A Power of Attorney authorizes one person (agent) to act on behalf of another (principal) for legal, financial, or property matters.
red_flags: used for property sale without registration
advice: A General POA for immovable property must be registered. Always specify scope clearly. Revoke immediately when no longer needed.

act: Indian Contract Act / Industrial Disputes Act
title: Contract of Employment
description: An employment contract defines the terms of work including salary, role, working hours, leave policy, and termination conditions between employer and employee.
advice: Non-compete clauses are largely unenforceable in India under Section 27. Ensure you receive appointment letter. Keep copies of all salary slips.

title: Partnership Agreement
description: A partnership deed defines profit/loss sharing ratio, roles, capital contribution, and dissolution terms among business partners.
advice: Register the partnership firm with the Registrar of Firms. An unregistered firm cannot sue third parties. Define exit terms clearly.

title: Non-Disclosure / Confidentiality Agreement
description: An NDA restricts parties from disclosing confidential business information to third parties for a specified period.
advice: Ensure confidential information is clearly defined. Perpetual NDAs are hard to enforce. Always include a breach remedy clause.

title: Construction / Builder-Buyer Agreement
description: A construction agreement between builder and buyer defines project specifications, payment schedule, possession date, and penalty for delay under RERA.
red_flags: super area instead of carpet area
advice: Verify RERA registration at your state RERA website. Builder must give possession as per agreement or pay interest at SBI MCLR + 2%. Carpet area is your legal right.

title: Last Will and Testament
description: A will is a legal declaration of how a person wishes to distribute their property after death. It requires two witnesses and can be registered.
advice: While registration is optional, a registered will is harder to contest. Appoint a trusted executor. Update will after major life events.

title: MOU / Letter of Intent
description: An MOU outlines the preliminary understanding between parties before a formal contract. It may or may not be legally binding depending on its language.
advice: Clearly state whether the MOU is binding or non-binding. A poorly drafted MOU can become enforceable. Always follow up with a formal agreement.
